# PROSPECTUS SUMMARY

This summary highlights selected information contained elsewhere in this prospectus and does not contain all of the information you should consider before investing in our Class A common stock. You should read this entire prospectus carefully, including the sections titled "Risk Factors," "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations," and our consolidated financial statements and the related notes included elsewhere in this prospectus, before making an investment decision. Unless the context otherwise requires, references in this prospectus to "Crestline," the "Company," "we," "us," and "our" refer to Crestline Software, Inc. and its consolidated subsidiary.

## Our Company

We are an enterprise software-as-a-service company providing an AI-driven supply chain optimization platform to Fortune 1000 manufacturers and retailers. Our platform, anchored by the Crestline Nexus platform and our proprietary SupplyMind AI engine, combines machine learning, advanced statistical modeling, and real-time data integration to deliver predictive and prescriptive supply chain intelligence, including demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and logistics routing.

We were incorporated in the State of Delaware on March 14, 2017 and are headquartered in Austin, Texas. We operate through our wholly-owned subsidiary, Crestline Software UK Ltd., incorporated in England and Wales, which supports our European operations, and we maintain additional offices in San Francisco, California; London, United Kingdom; and Bangalore, India. As of December 31, 2024, we had 1,247 employees.

Our intellectual property portfolio includes 47 issued U.S. patents, 12 pending U.S. patent applications, and 8 issued foreign patents covering core algorithmic innovations and data processing methods. We have registered the trademarks "Crestline," "Crestline Nexus," and "SupplyMind" with the United States Patent and Trademark Office. We achieved SOC 2 Type II certification in August 2024 and maintain compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA).

## Our Industry and Market Opportunity

We operate in the market for AI-driven supply chain optimization software. # PROSPECTUS SUMMARY

This summary highlights selected information contained elsewhere in this prospectus and does not contain all of the information you should consider before investing in our Class A common stock. You should read this entire prospectus carefully, including the sections titled "Risk Factors," "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations," and our consolidated financial statements and the related notes included elsewhere in this prospectus, before making an investment decision. ## Our Company

We are an enterprise software-as-a-service company providing an AI-driven supply chain optimization platform to Fortune 1000 manufacturers and retailers. Our platform, anchored by the Crestline Nexus platform and our proprietary SupplyMind AI engine, combines machine learning, advanced statistical modeling, and real-time data integration to deliver predictive and prescriptive supply chain intelligence, including demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and logistics routing.

We were incorporated in the State of Delaware on March 14, 2017 and are headquartered in Austin, Texas. We operate through our wholly-owned subsidiary, Crestline Software UK Ltd., incorporated in England and Wales, which supports our European operations, and we maintain additional offices in San Francisco, California; London, United Kingdom; and Bangalore, India. As of December 31, 2024, we had 1,247 employees.

Our intellectual property portfolio includes 47 issued U.S. patents, 12 pending U.S. patent applications, and 8 issued foreign patents covering core algorithmic innovations and data processing methods. We have registered the trademarks "Crestline," "Crestline Nexus," and "SupplyMind" with the United States Patent and Trademark Office. # RISK FACTORS

Investing in our Class A common stock involves a high degree of risk. Before making an investment decision, you should carefully consider the risks and uncertainties described below, together with the other information contained in this prospectus, including our consolidated financial statements and the related notes. The occurrence of any of the following risks could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and prospects, and the trading price of our Class A common stock could decline, and you could lose all or part of your investment. Additional risks and uncertainties not presently known to us, or that we currently deem immaterial, may also impair our business.

## Risks Related to Our Business and Industry

**We have a history of net losses and may not be able to achieve or sustain profitability.**

We have incurred net losses since inception. We reported a net loss of $24.8 million for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 and $27.4 million for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023. As of December 31, 2024, we had an accumulated deficit of $312.6 million. We expect to continue to incur operating losses and negative operating cash flows for the foreseeable future as we invest in research and development, sales and marketing, and international expansion. We may not achieve or sustain profitability, and our losses could be larger than expected.

**We may not be able to sustain our historical revenue growth rate.**

Our revenue grew 45.3% from $128.9 million in fiscal year 2023 to $187.3 million in fiscal year 2024. You should not rely on our historical revenue growth as an indication of future performance. Our revenue growth rate may decline as our revenue base increases, as a result of increased competition, or for other reasons.

**Our dollar-based net retention rate and other operating metrics may not accurately predict our future results.**

Our dollar-based net retention rate was 132% for fiscal year 2024. This metric is calculated based on our internal records and may not accurately reflect the metrics that would be derived from a more rigorous or standardized methodology. Any inability to maintain a high net retention rate would adversely affect our growth.

**We depend on a limited number of customers for a significant portion of our revenue.**

No single customer accounted for more than 10% of total revenue in fiscal year 2024 or 2023; however, our largest customer, Harmon Industries Inc., represented approximately 8.2% of total revenue ($15.4 million) in fiscal year 2024, and our top 10 customers collectively represented 31.7% of total revenue ($59.4 million). The loss of, or a significant reduction in business from, one or more of these customers could materially and adversely affect our revenue and results of operations.

**We face intense competition and may lack sufficient financial resources to compete effectively.**

The market for supply chain optimization software is competitive and includes well-capitalized legacy enterprise resource planning vendors and point-solution providers. Some competitors have greater financial, technical, and marketing resources than we do. We may not be able to compete effectively.

**If we fail to maintain the security of our platform or our customers' data, our business and reputation would be harmed.**

Our platform processes sensitive customer supply chain data. Any security breach, cyberattack, or failure to maintain SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, or CCPA compliance could result in liability, reputational harm, and loss of customers.

**We are subject to pending patent infringement litigation that could result in significant damages and adversely affect our business.**

On September 12, 2024, Optivance, Inc. filed a patent infringement lawsuit against us in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Texas (Case No. 6:24-cv-00831), alleging that certain features of our SupplyMind platform infringe U.S. Patent No. 11,284,107, relating to "machine-learning-based inventory forecasting." We believe the claims are without merit and intend to vigorously defend against them. However, litigation is inherently uncertain. Based on the advice of outside patent counsel, we estimate the potential range of loss, in the event of an adverse judgment, to be between $5.0 million and $20.0 million, although we consider an unfavorable outcome to have a low probability. We have not recorded a loss contingency for this matter. An adverse judgment could also trigger an event of default under our term loan if a judgment in excess of $5.0 million remains undischarged for 60 days. See "Legal Proceedings" and "Description of Indebtedness."

**Our intellectual property is pledged as collateral under our term loan, which could impair our ability to operate our business in the event of a lender foreclosure.**

Our obligations under our term loan are secured by a first-priority security interest in substantially all of our assets, including our intellectual property (the 47 issued U.S. patents, 12 pending applications, 8 foreign patents, and our registered trademarks). If we were to default under the term loan and the lender exercised its remedies, the lender could foreclose on our intellectual property, which would severely disrupt our business. See "Description of Indebtedness."

**We depend on key personnel, including our co-founders.**

Our success depends on the continued services of our co-founders, Arun Ramaswamy, our Chief Executive Officer, and Katerina "Kate" Litvak, our Chief Technology Officer. The loss of the services of either co-founder could adversely affect our business. Our term loan also contains a key-person provision that constitutes a change of control if Mr. Ramaswamy ceases to serve as Chief Executive Officer and a successor acceptable to the lender is not appointed within 90 days.

**Our ability to use our net operating loss carryforwards to offset future taxable income may be subject to limitations.**

As of December 31, 2024, we had approximately $228.0 million of federal net operating loss carryforwards and approximately $166.0 million of state net operating loss carryforwards. Our ability to use these carryforwards may be limited if we experience an "ownership change" as defined in Section 382 of the Internal Revenue Code, including in connection with this offering or other equity transactions. We have not yet completed a Section 382 study. In addition, we maintain a full valuation allowance against our net deferred tax assets.

**We have identified a material weakness in our internal control over financial reporting that we are in the process of remediating.** [Drafting note: confirm with management and auditor whether any material weaknesses have been identified; if none, remove this risk factor.]

## Risks Related to Our Indebtedness

**The terms of our term loan impose operating and financial restrictions on us and may limit our flexibility.**

Our term loan with Kestridge West Credit Partners contains financial covenants, including a minimum liquidity requirement of $30.0 million and an annual recurring revenue-based leverage ratio not to exceed 2.0x. As of December 31, 2024, we were in compliance with all financial covenants, with liquidity of $137.0 million and a leverage ratio of approximately 0.19x. Our failure to comply with these covenants could result in an event of default and acceleration of our indebtedness.

**We are required to provide our lender with prior notice of this offering.**

Our term loan agreement requires us to provide Kestridge West Credit Partners with written notice not less than 30 days prior to the consummation of any initial public offering of our equity securities, including the anticipated date, gross proceeds, use of proceeds, and a pro forma capitalization table. Failure to provide this notice within the required timeframe would constitute an event of default, subject to a 10-business-day cure period. See "Description of Indebtedness."

**A change of control under our term loan could result in acceleration of our indebtedness.**

Our term loan agreement contains a change of control provision. Based on the dual-class capital structure to be adopted upon the closing of this offering, our existing stockholders are expected to retain more than 50% of the aggregate voting power following this offering, and we do not believe this offering will constitute a change of control. However, the interpretation of this provision is complex, and we cannot assure you that the lender will agree with our analysis. See "Description of Indebtedness."

## Risks Related to Our Common Stock and This Offering

**The dual-class structure of our common stock and the concentrated voting power of our co-founders will limit your ability to influence corporate matters.**

Upon the closing of this offering, our amended and restated certificate of incorporation will provide for a dual-class common stock structure consisting of Class A common stock (one vote per share) and Class B common stock (ten votes per share). Our co-founders will hold all 20,800,000 shares of Class B common stock and, together, will control approximately 71.8% of the voting power of our outstanding capital stock. As a result, the co-founders will have the ability to control the outcome of matters submitted to our stockholders for approval, including the election of directors, amendments to our certificate of incorporation, and any merger, consolidation, or sale of substantially all of our assets. This concentration of voting power will limit your ability to influence corporate matters and may adversely affect the market price of our Class A common stock.

**Our Class B common stock has no time-based or ownership-based sunset provision.**

Our Class B common stock will convert into Class A common stock only upon (i) transfer to a non-affiliate or (ii) the death or incapacity of the holder. There is no time-based sunset (such as a fixed period after this offering) and no ownership-percentage-based sunset. As a result, the dual-class structure and the concentrated voting power of our co-founders will continue indefinitely. This structure may cause our Class A common stock to be excluded from certain stock market indices, such as the S&P 500 and the FTSE Russell 1000, which have adopted policies restricting companies with multi-class share structures, which could reduce demand for and the market price of our Class A common stock.

**Because we will be a "controlled company" under Nasdaq rules, we will be permitted to rely on exemptions from certain corporate governance requirements.**

Because our co-founders will control more than 50% of the voting power of our outstanding capital stock, we will be a "controlled company" under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5615(c). We intend to rely on the exemptions available to controlled companies from the requirement that a majority of our board of directors consist of independent directors, the requirement that our compensation committee be comprised entirely of independent directors, and the requirement that director nominees be selected by independent directors or a nominating committee comprised entirely of independent directors. As a result, you will not have the same protections afforded to stockholders of companies that are subject to these requirements. Our audit committee will remain comprised entirely of independent directors, as the controlled company exemptions do not apply to audit committee independence.

**Anti-takeover provisions in our charter and bylaws could make it more difficult for a third party to acquire us, even if doing so would benefit our stockholders.**

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation and bylaws will contain provisions that may delay, deter, or prevent a change of control or changes in our management that a stockholder might consider favorable, including: a classified (staggered) board of directors; the ability of our board of directors to fill vacancies; removal of directors only for cause and only upon the affirmative vote of the holders of at least 66⅔% of the voting power; the prohibition on stockholder action by written consent (other than by Class B holders acting separately as a class); the inability of stockholders to call special meetings; advance notice requirements for stockholder proposals and nominations; and supermajority voting requirements to amend certain provisions of our charter and bylaws. These provisions could limit the price that investors might be willing to pay in the future for our Class A common stock.

**Our exclusive forum provisions may limit your ability to obtain a favorable judicial venue.**

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation will provide that, unless we consent in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware will be the sole and exclusive forum for certain state-law claims, and the federal district courts of the United States will be the sole and exclusive forum for claims arising under the Securities Act. These provisions may limit a stockholder's ability to bring a claim in a judicial forum that it finds favorable for disputes with us or our directors, officers, or stockholders, which may discourage such lawsuits.

**Our corporate opportunities provision may allocate certain business opportunities to our directors who are affiliated with venture capital or private equity funds.**

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation will renounce our interest in certain business opportunities presented to directors who are also partners, members, or employees of investment funds, venture capital funds, or private equity funds, including our directors affiliated with Graymont Ventures and Ridgeline Growth Partners. This means that such directors may pursue business opportunities that might otherwise be available to us, which could reduce the business opportunities available to us.

**You will experience immediate and substantial dilution in the pro forma net tangible book value per share of the Class A common stock you purchase.**

The assumed initial public offering price of $24.00 per share (the midpoint of the estimated range) is substantially higher than the pro forma net tangible book value per share of our Class A common stock. As a result, you will experience immediate and substantial dilution of $19.85 per share. See "Dilution."

**Future sales of our Class A common stock, or the perception that such sales may occur, could cause the market price of our Class A common stock to decline.**

Substantial numbers of shares of our Class A common stock will be eligible for sale in the public market after this offering, including shares issuable upon the exercise of outstanding stock options and the vesting and settlement of outstanding RSUs, subject to lock-up agreements and other restrictions. Sales of a substantial number of shares, or the perception that such sales may occur, could cause the market price of our Class A common stock to decline.

**We do not intend to pay dividends on our Class A common stock, and you must rely on price appreciation for a return on your investment.**

We have never declared or paid any cash dividends on our capital stock. We do not intend to pay cash dividends in the foreseeable future. In addition, our term loan agreement prohibits the payment of dividends and stock repurchases without the lender's consent. Any return on your investment will depend on the future appreciation of our Class A common stock, which may not occur.

# RISK FACTORS

Investing in our Class A common stock involves a high degree of risk. Before making an investment decision, you should carefully consider the risks and uncertainties described below, together with the other information contained in this prospectus, including our consolidated financial statements and the related notes. The occurrence of any of the following risks could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and prospects, and the trading price of our Class A common stock could decline, and you could lose all or part of your investment. Additional risks and uncertainties not presently known to us, or that we currently deem immaterial, may also impair our business.

## Risks Related to Our Business and Industry

**We have a history of net losses and may not be able to achieve or sustain profitability.**

We have incurred net losses since inception. We reported a net loss of $24.8 million for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 and $27.4 million for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023. As of December 31, 2024, we had an accumulated deficit of $312.6 million. We expect to continue to incur operating losses and negative operating cash flows for the foreseeable future as we invest in research and development, sales and marketing, and international expansion. We may not achieve or sustain profitability, and our losses could be larger than expected.

**We may not be able to sustain our historical revenue growth rate.**

Our revenue grew 45.3% from $128.9 million in fiscal year 2023 to $187.3 million in fiscal year 2024. You should not rely on our historical revenue growth as an indication of future performance. Our revenue growth rate may decline as our revenue base increases, as a result of increased competition, or for other reasons.

**Our dollar-based net retention rate and other operating metrics may not accurately predict our future results.**

Our dollar-based net retention rate was 132% for fiscal year 2024. This metric is calculated based on our internal records and may not accurately reflect the metrics that would be derived from a more rigorous or standardized methodology. Any inability to maintain a high net retention rate would adversely affect our growth.

**We depend on a limited number of customers for a significant portion of our revenue.**

No single customer accounted for more than 10% of total revenue in fiscal year 2024 or 2023; however, our largest customer, Harmon Industries Inc., represented approximately 8.2% of total revenue ($15.4 million) in fiscal year 2024, and our top 10 customers collectively represented 31.7% of total revenue ($59.4 million). The loss of, or a significant reduction in business from, one or more of these customers could materially and adversely affect our revenue and results of operations.

**We face intense competition and may lack sufficient financial resources to compete effectively.**

The market for supply chain optimization software is competitive and includes well-capitalized legacy enterprise resource planning vendors and point-solution providers. Some competitors have greater financial, technical, and marketing resources than we do. We may not be able to compete effectively.

**If we fail to maintain the security of our platform or our customers' data, our business and reputation would be harmed.**

Our platform processes sensitive customer supply chain data. Any security breach, cyberattack, or failure to maintain SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, or CCPA compliance could result in liability, reputational harm, and loss of customers.

**We are subject to pending patent infringement litigation that could result in significant damages and adversely affect our business.**

On September 12, 2024, Optivance, Inc. filed a patent infringement lawsuit against us in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Texas (Case No. 6:24-cv-00831), alleging that certain features of our SupplyMind platform infringe U.S. Patent No. 11,284,107, relating to "machine-learning-based inventory forecasting." We believe the claims are without merit and intend to vigorously defend against them. However, litigation is inherently uncertain. Based on the advice of outside patent counsel, we estimate the potential range of loss, in the event of an adverse judgment, to be between $5.0 million and $20.0 million, although we consider an unfavorable outcome to have a low probability. We have not recorded a loss contingency for this matter. An adverse judgment could also trigger an event of default under our term loan if a judgment in excess of $5.0 million remains undischarged for 60 days. See "Legal Proceedings" and "Description of Indebtedness."

**Our intellectual property is pledged as collateral under our term loan, which could impair our ability to operate our business in the event of a lender foreclosure.**

Our obligations under our term loan are secured by a first-priority security interest in substantially all of our assets, including our intellectual property (the 47 issued U.S. patents, 12 pending applications, 8 foreign patents, and our registered trademarks). If we were to default under the term loan and the lender exercised its remedies, the lender could foreclose on our intellectual property, which would severely disrupt our business. See "Description of Indebtedness."

**We depend on key personnel, including our co-founders.**

Our success depends on the continued services of our co-founders, Arun Ramaswamy, our Chief Executive Officer, and Katerina "Kate" Litvak, our Chief Technology Officer. The loss of the services of either co-founder could adversely affect our business. Our term loan also contains a key-person provision that constitutes a change of control if Mr. Ramaswamy ceases to serve as Chief Executive Officer and a successor acceptable to the lender is not appointed within 90 days.

**Our ability to use our net operating loss carryforwards to offset future taxable income may be subject to limitations.**

As of December 31, 2024, we had approximately $228.0 million of federal net operating loss carryforwards and approximately $166.0 million of state net operating loss carryforwards. Our ability to use these carryforwards may be limited if we experience an "ownership change" as defined in Section 382 of the Internal Revenue Code, including in connection with this offering or other equity transactions. We have not yet completed a Section 382 study. In addition, we maintain a full valuation allowance against our net deferred tax assets.

**We have identified a material weakness in our internal control over financial reporting that we are in the process of remediating.** [Drafting note: confirm with management and auditor whether any material weaknesses have been identified; if none, remove this risk factor.]

## Risks Related to Our Indebtedness

**The terms of our term loan impose operating and financial restrictions on us and may limit our flexibility.**

Our term loan with Kestridge West Credit Partners contains financial covenants, including a minimum liquidity requirement of $30.0 million and an annual recurring revenue-based leverage ratio not to exceed 2.0x. As of December 31, 2024, we were in compliance with all financial covenants, with liquidity of $137.0 million and a leverage ratio of approximately 0.19x. Our failure to comply with these covenants could result in an event of default and acceleration of our indebtedness.

**We are required to provide our lender with prior notice of this offering.**

Our term loan agreement requires us to provide Kestridge West Credit Partners with written notice not less than 30 days prior to the consummation of any initial public offering of our equity securities, including the anticipated date, gross proceeds, use of proceeds, and a pro forma capitalization table. Failure to provide this notice within the required timeframe would constitute an event of default, subject to a 10-business-day cure period. See "Description of Indebtedness."

**A change of control under our term loan could result in acceleration of our indebtedness.**

Our term loan agreement contains a change of control provision. Based on the dual-class capital structure to be adopted upon the closing of this offering, our existing stockholders are expected to retain more than 50% of the aggregate voting power following this offering, and we do not believe this offering will constitute a change of control. However, the interpretation of this provision is complex, and we cannot assure you that the lender will agree with our analysis. See "Description of Indebtedness."

## Risks Related to Our Common Stock and This Offering

**The dual-class structure of our common stock and the concentrated voting power of our co-founders will limit your ability to influence corporate matters.**

Upon the closing of this offering, our amended and restated certificate of incorporation will provide for a dual-class common stock structure consisting of Class A common stock (one vote per share) and Class B common stock (ten votes per share). Our co-founders will hold all 20,800,000 shares of Class B common stock and, together, will control approximately 71.8% of the voting power of our outstanding capital stock. As a result, the co-founders will have the ability to control the outcome of matters submitted to our stockholders for approval, including the election of directors, amendments to our certificate of incorporation, and any merger, consolidation, or sale of substantially all of our assets. This concentration of voting power will limit your ability to influence corporate matters and may adversely affect the market price of our Class A common stock.

**Our Class B common stock has no time-based or ownership-based sunset provision.**

Our Class B common stock will convert into Class A common stock only upon (i) transfer to a non-affiliate or (ii) the death or incapacity of the holder. There is no time-based sunset (such as a fixed period after this offering) and no ownership-percentage-based sunset. As a result, the dual-class structure and the concentrated voting power of our co-founders will continue indefinitely. This structure may cause our Class A common stock to be excluded from certain stock market indices, such as the S&P 500 and the FTSE Russell 1000, which have adopted policies restricting companies with multi-class share structures, which could reduce demand for and the market price of our Class A common stock.

**Because we will be a "controlled company" under Nasdaq rules, we will be permitted to rely on exemptions from certain corporate governance requirements.**

Because our co-founders will control more than 50% of the voting power of our outstanding capital stock, we will be a "controlled company" under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5615(c). We intend to rely on the exemptions available to controlled companies from the requirement that a majority of our board of directors consist of independent directors, the requirement that our compensation committee be comprised entirely of independent directors, and the requirement that director nominees be selected by independent directors or a nominating committee comprised entirely of independent directors. As a result, you will not have the same protections afforded to stockholders of companies that are subject to these requirements. Our audit committee will remain comprised entirely of independent directors, as the controlled company exemptions do not apply to audit committee independence.

**Anti-takeover provisions in our charter and bylaws could make it more difficult for a third party to acquire us, even if doing so would benefit our stockholders.**

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation and bylaws will contain provisions that may delay, deter, or prevent a change of control or changes in our management that a stockholder might consider favorable, including: a classified (staggered) board of directors; the ability of our board of directors to fill vacancies; removal of directors only for cause and only upon the affirmative vote of the holders of at least 66⅔% of the voting power; the prohibition on stockholder action by written consent (other than by Class B holders acting separately as a class); the inability of stockholders to call special meetings; advance notice requirements for stockholder proposals and nominations; and supermajority voting requirements to amend certain provisions of our charter and bylaws. These provisions could limit the price that investors might be willing to pay in the future for our Class A common stock.

**Our exclusive forum provisions may limit your ability to obtain a favorable judicial venue.**

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation will provide that, unless we consent in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware will be the sole and exclusive forum for certain state-law claims, and the federal district courts of the United States will be the sole and exclusive forum for claims arising under the Securities Act. These provisions may limit a stockholder's ability to bring a claim in a judicial forum that it finds favorable for disputes with us or our directors, officers, or stockholders, which may discourage such lawsuits.

**Our corporate opportunities provision may allocate certain business opportunities to our directors who are affiliated with venture capital or private equity funds.**

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation will renounce our interest in certain business opportunities presented to directors who are also partners, members, or employees of investment funds, venture capital funds, or private equity funds, including our directors affiliated with Graymont Ventures and Ridgeline Growth Partners. This means that such directors may pursue business opportunities that might otherwise be available to us, which could reduce the business opportunities available to us.

**You will experience immediate and substantial dilution in the pro forma net tangible book value per share of the Class A common stock you purchase.**

The assumed initial public offering price of $24.00 per share (the midpoint of the estimated range) is substantially higher than the pro forma net tangible book value per share of our Class A common stock. As a result, you will experience immediate and substantial dilution of $19.85 per share. See "Dilution."

**Future sales of our Class A common stock, or the perception that such sales may occur, could cause the market price of our Class A common stock to decline.**

Substantial numbers of shares of our Class A common stock will be eligible for sale in the public market after this offering, including shares issuable upon the exercise of outstanding stock options and the vesting and settlement of outstanding RSUs, subject to lock-up agreements and other restrictions. Sales of a substantial number of shares, or the perception that such sales may occur, could cause the market price of our Class A common stock to decline.

**We do not intend to pay dividends on our Class A common stock, and you must rely on price appreciation for a return on your investment.**

We have never declared or paid any cash dividends on our capital stock. We do not intend to pay cash dividends in the foreseeable future. In addition, our term loan agreement prohibits the payment of dividends and stock repurchases without the lender's consent. Any return on your investment will depend on the future appreciation of our Class A common stock, which may not occur.

# DILUTION

The net tangible book value per share of our common stock is the amount of our total tangible assets less total liabilities, divided by the number of shares of common stock outstanding. Dilution in net tangible book value per share represents the difference between the amount per share paid by purchasers of Class A common stock in this offering and the pro forma net tangible book value per share of Class A common stock immediately after this offering.

Our historical net tangible book value as of December 31, 2024 was a deficit of $(2.85) per share, calculated as total stockholders' deficit of $(288.0) million divided by 52,000,000 shares of common stock outstanding.

After giving effect to (i) the automatic conversion of all 35,416,667 outstanding shares of redeemable convertible preferred stock into an equal number of shares of Class A common stock and the reclassification of $385.0 million from mezzanine equity to permanent stockholders' equity, and (ii) the issuance and sale of [●] shares of Class A common stock in this offering at an assumed initial public offering price of $24.00 per share (the midpoint of the estimated range), after deducting the underwriting discounts and commissions and estimated offering expenses payable by us, our pro forma net tangible book value as of December 31, 2024 would have been approximately $**4.15** per share. This represents an immediate increase in pro forma net tangible book value of $**3.09** per share to our existing stockholders and immediate dilution of $**19.85** per share to purchasers of Class A common stock in this offering, as illustrated in the following table:

| | |
|---|---|
| Assumed initial public offering price per share | $24.00 |
| Pro forma net tangible book value per share before this offering | $1.06 |
| Increase per share attributable to this offering | $3.09 |
| Pro forma net tangible book value per share after this offering | $4.15 |
| Dilution per share to new investors | $19.85 |

The pro forma net tangible book value per share before this offering of $1.06 was calculated as (total assets of $240.1 million less intangible assets of $4.1 million less total liabilities of $143.1 million) divided by 87,416,667 shares (52,000,000 shares of common stock plus 35,416,667 shares of Class A common stock issuable upon conversion of redeemable convertible preferred stock). The pro forma net tangible book value per share after this offering of $4.15 was calculated as ($92.9 million plus estimated net proceeds of $331.8 million) divided by 102,416,667 shares (87,416,667 shares plus [●] shares of Class A common stock issued in this offering).

If the underwriters exercise their over-allotment option in full, the pro forma net tangible book value per share after this offering would be approximately $**4.54** per share, and the dilution to new investors would be approximately $**19.46** per share.

The foregoing dilution calculations do not give effect to (i) [●] shares of Class A common stock issuable upon the exercise of outstanding stock options with a weighted-average exercise price of $8.42 per share, (ii) [●] shares of Class A common stock issuable upon vesting and settlement of outstanding RSUs, (iii) [●] shares available for future grant under our 2024 Equity Incentive Plan, or (iv) [●] shares reserved under our 2024 Employee Stock Purchase Plan. The exercise of outstanding options or the vesting and settlement of outstanding RSUs, and the issuance of shares under our equity plans, will cause further dilution to new investors.

[Drafting note: The Company's internal materials state that, if the over-allotment option is exercised in full, the pro forma net tangible book value per share would be approximately $4.36 and dilution would be approximately $19.64. Based on additional net proceeds of approximately $50.5 million from the over-allotment option, the correct pro forma net tangible book value per share is approximately $4.54 and dilution is approximately $19.46. This discrepancy must be corrected.]

---

# PLAN OF DISTRIBUTION

We have entered into an underwriting agreement, dated [●], 2025, with Pinnacle Ridge Capital, as representative of the several underwriters named in the underwriting agreement, relating to the shares of Class A common stock being offered by this prospectus. Pinnacle Ridge Capital is acting as lead left book-running manager, and Stonebridge Greer & Co. is acting as co-book-running manager. Subject to the terms and conditions of the underwriting agreement, the underwriters have agreed to purchase all of the shares of Class A common stock offered by this prospectus, other than the shares covered by the over-allotment option described below, at a price equal to the initial public offering price less the underwriting discounts and commissions.

The underwriting discounts and commissions will be 6.5% of the initial public offering price. The 6.5% discount is allocated as follows: a management fee of 20% of the discount, an underwriting fee of 20% of the discount, and a selling concession of 60% of the discount. The allocation of the discount between Pinnacle Ridge Capital and Stonebridge Greer & Co. will be set forth in the underwriting agreement.

The underwriters will have an option to purchase up to an additional [●] shares of Class A common stock from us at the initial public offering price, less the underwriting discounts and commissions, for a period of 30 days after the date of this prospectus, solely to cover over-allotments, if any.

The following table shows the per share and total underwriting discounts and commissions to be paid to the underwriters by us, assuming no exercise and full exercise of the over-allotment option, at the midpoint of the estimated price range:

| | No Over-Allotment | Full Over-Allotment |
|---|---|---|
| Per share | $1.56 | $1.56 |
| Total | $[●] | $[●] |

[Drafting note: At the midpoint price of $24.00, the per-share discount is $1.56 (6.5% of $24.00); the total discount is $23.4 million on the base offering and $26.91 million if the over-allotment option is exercised in full.]

**Lock-Up Agreements.** Each of our executive officers, directors, and stockholders beneficially owning 5% or more of our outstanding capital stock immediately prior to this offering has agreed, subject to certain exceptions, not to offer, sell, contract to sell, pledge, or otherwise dispose of any shares of our capital stock or any securities convertible into or exercisable for our capital stock for a period of 180 days after the date of the final prospectus, without the prior written consent of Pinnacle Ridge Capital. The lock-up agreements bind the fund-level entities that hold shares attributable to our directors affiliated with Graymont Ventures and Ridgeline Growth Partners. Pinnacle Ridge Capital may, in its sole discretion, release any of these lock-up restrictions at any time.

**Stabilization.** In connection with this offering, the underwriters may engage in stabilizing transactions, short sales, and the imposition of penalty bids in accordance with Regulation M under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. These activities may have the effect of raising or maintaining the market price of our Class A common stock above independent market levels or preventing or retarding a decline in the market price.

**Listing.** We have applied to list our Class A common stock on The Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol "CRST."

**Transfer Agent and Registrar.** The transfer agent and registrar for our Class A common stock will be [●]. [Drafting note: The Company's bylaws name Computershare Transfer Co. as transfer agent, while the underwriting term sheet and management questionnaire refer to Oakvale Transfer Transfer Co. The correct transfer agent must be confirmed and the bylaws updated accordingly.]

**Emerging Growth Company Status.** We are an "emerging growth company" as defined in the JOBS Act and will avail ourselves of certain reduced disclosure and reporting requirements available to emerging growth companies.

**FINRA.** The terms of this offering will comply with FINRA Rule 5110 regarding underwriting compensation.

This represents an immediate increase in pro forma net tangible book value of $**3.09** per share to our existing stockholders and immediate dilution of $**19.85** per share to purchasers of Class A common stock in this offering, as illustrated in the following table:

| | |
|---|---|
| Assumed initial public offering price per share | $24.00 |
| Pro forma net tangible book value per share before this offering | $1.06 |
| Increase per share attributable to this offering | $3.09 |
| Pro forma net tangible book value per share after this offering | $4.15 |
| Dilution per share to new investors | $19.85 |

The pro forma net tangible book value per share before this offering of $1.06 was calculated as (total assets of $240.1 million less intangible assets of $4.1 million less total liabilities of $143.1 million) divided by 87,416,667 shares (52,000,000 shares of common stock plus 35,416,667 shares of Class A common stock issuable upon conversion of redeemable convertible preferred stock). The pro forma net tangible book value per share after this offering of $4.15 was calculated as ($92.9 million plus estimated net proceeds of $331.8 million) divided by 102,416,667 shares (87,416,667 shares plus [●] shares of Class A common stock issued in this offering).

If the underwriters exercise their over-allotment option in full, the pro forma net tangible book value per share after this offering would be approximately $**4.54** per share, and the dilution to new investors would be approximately $**19.46** per share.

The foregoing dilution calculations do not give effect to (i) [●] shares of Class A common stock issuable upon the exercise of outstanding stock options with a weighted-average exercise price of $8.42 per share, (ii) [●] shares of Class A common stock issuable upon vesting and settlement of outstanding RSUs, (iii) [●] shares available for future grant under our 2024 Equity Incentive Plan, or (iv) [●] shares reserved under our 2024 Employee Stock Purchase Plan. The exercise of outstanding options or the vesting and settlement of outstanding RSUs, and the issuance of shares under our equity plans, will cause further dilution to new investors.

[Drafting note: The Company's internal materials state that, if the over-allotment option is exercised in full, the pro forma net tangible book value per share would be approximately $4.36 and dilution would be approximately $19.64. Based on additional net proceeds of approximately $50.5 million from the over-allotment option, the correct pro forma net tangible book value per share is approximately $4.54 and dilution is approximately $19.46. This discrepancy must be corrected.]

# PLAN OF DISTRIBUTION

We have entered into an underwriting agreement, dated [●], 2025, with Pinnacle Ridge Capital, as representative of the several underwriters named in the underwriting agreement, relating to the shares of Class A common stock being offered by this prospectus. Pinnacle Ridge Capital is acting as lead left book-running manager, and Stonebridge Greer & Co. is acting as co-book-running manager. Subject to the terms and conditions of the underwriting agreement, the underwriters have agreed to purchase all of the shares of Class A common stock offered by this prospectus, other than the shares covered by the over-allotment option described below, at a price equal to the initial public offering price less the underwriting discounts and commissions.

The underwriting discounts and commissions will be 6.5% of the initial public offering price. The 6.5% discount is allocated as follows: a management fee of 20% of the discount, an underwriting fee of 20% of the discount, and a selling concession of 60% of the discount. The allocation of the discount between Pinnacle Ridge Capital and Stonebridge Greer & Co. will be set forth in the underwriting agreement.

The underwriters will have an option to purchase up to an additional [●] shares of Class A common stock from us at the initial public offering price, less the underwriting discounts and commissions, for a period of 30 days after the date of this prospectus, solely to cover over-allotments, if any.

The following table shows the per share and total underwriting discounts and commissions to be paid to the underwriters by us, assuming no exercise and full exercise of the over-allotment option, at the midpoint of the estimated price range:

| | No Over-Allotment | Full Over-Allotment |
|---|---|---|
| Per share | $1.56 | $1.56 |
| Total | $[●] | $[●] |

**Lock-Up Agreements.** Each of our executive officers, directors, and stockholders beneficially owning 5% or more of our outstanding capital stock immediately prior to this offering has agreed, subject to certain exceptions, not to offer, sell, contract to sell, pledge, or otherwise dispose of any shares of our capital stock or any securities convertible into or exercisable for our capital stock for a period of 180 days after the date of the final prospectus, without the prior written consent of Pinnacle Ridge Capital. The lock-up agreements bind the fund-level entities that hold shares attributable to our directors affiliated with Graymont Ventures and Ridgeline Growth Partners. Pinnacle Ridge Capital may, in its sole discretion, release any of these lock-up restrictions at any time.

These activities may have the effect of raising or maintaining the market price of our Class A common stock above independent market levels or preventing or retarding a decline in the market price.

The correct transfer agent must be confirmed and the bylaws updated accordingly.]

# DESCRIPTION OF CAPITAL STOCK

The following is a summary of the material terms of our capital stock. This summary is not complete and is qualified in its entirety by reference to our amended and restated certificate of incorporation and amended and restated bylaws, copies of which are filed as exhibits to this registration statement.

## General

Upon the closing of this offering, our amended and restated certificate of incorporation will become effective. It will authorize 1,500,000,000 shares of capital stock, consisting of (a) 1,200,000,000 shares of Class A common stock, par value $0.0001 per share; (b) 200,000,000 shares of Class B common stock, par value $0.0001 per share; and (c) 100,000,000 shares of preferred stock, par value $0.0001 per share. Upon the closing of this offering, all 35,416,667 outstanding shares of our Series A, Series B, Series C, and Series D redeemable convertible preferred stock will automatically convert into an equal number of shares of Class A common stock, and no shares of preferred stock will remain outstanding.

## Class A Common Stock

Holders of Class A common stock are entitled to one vote per share on all matters submitted to a vote of stockholders, voting together with the holders of Class B common stock as a single class, except as otherwise required by law. Holders of Class A common stock are entitled to receive dividends and distributions when, as, and if declared by our board of directors, on an equal per share basis with holders of Class B common stock. In the event of a liquidation, dissolution, or winding up, holders of Class A common stock are entitled to share ratably in the assets remaining after payment of liabilities and any amounts due to holders of preferred stock, on an equal per share basis with holders of Class B common stock. Class A common stock has no conversion, redemption, or sinking fund rights.

## Class B Common Stock

Holders of Class B common stock are entitled to ten votes per share on all matters submitted to a vote of stockholders, voting together with the holders of Class A common stock as a single class, except as otherwise required by law. Holders of Class B common stock are entitled to receive dividends and distributions on an equal per share basis with holders of Class A common stock, and to share ratably in the assets remaining upon liquidation on an equal per share basis with holders of Class A common stock.

Each share of Class B common stock is convertible at any time at the option of the holder into one share of Class A common stock. Each share of Class B common stock will automatically convert into one share of Class A common stock upon (i) any transfer of such share to a person or entity other than a permitted transferee, or (ii) the death or incapacity of the holder, if the holder is a natural person. "Permitted transferee" includes the holder's spouse, domestic partner, parents, children, grandchildren, and trusts established for the benefit of such persons; entities wholly owned by the holder or permitted transferees; and transfers between the two co-founders. There is no time-based or ownership-percentage-based sunset on the Class B common stock.

Upon the closing of this offering, our co-founders will hold all 20,800,000 shares of Class B common stock: Arun Ramaswamy will hold 12,200,000 shares and Katerina "Kate" Litvak will hold 8,600,000 shares. As a result, our co-founders will together control approximately 71.8% of the voting power of our outstanding capital stock.

## Preferred Stock

Our board of directors will have the authority, subject to limitations prescribed by law, to issue shares of preferred stock in one or more series and to fix the designation, powers, preferences, and rights of each series without any further vote or action by the stockholders. The issuance of preferred stock could adversely affect the voting power of holders of Class A common stock and the likelihood that such holders will receive dividend payments and distributions upon liquidation.

## Registration Rights

In June 2023, we entered into an Investor Rights Agreement with certain holders of our preferred stock. Pursuant to the Investor Rights Agreement, these holders are entitled to demand registration rights and S-3 piggyback registration rights, commencing 180 days after the closing of this offering, subject to the terms and conditions of the agreement. The Voting Agreement and Right of First Refusal and Co-Sale Agreement entered into in connection with the Series D financing will terminate upon the closing of this offering.

## Anti-Takeover Effects of Provisions of Our Charter and Bylaws

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation and bylaws will contain provisions that could have anti-takeover effects, including:

- **Classified board.** Our board of directors will be divided into three classes, with staggered three-year terms. The initial classes will be: Class I (Priya Venkatesh and Thomas J. Moreno, terms expiring at the 2026 annual meeting); Class II (Dr. Helen Park and James Kwon, terms expiring at the 2027 annual meeting); and Class III (Arun Ramaswamy, Katerina "Kate" Litvak, and Adaora Obi, terms expiring at the 2028 annual meeting).
- **Removal of directors.** Directors may be removed only for cause and only upon the affirmative vote of the holders of at least 66⅔% of the voting power of all outstanding shares of capital stock.
- **Vacancies.** Vacancies on our board of directors will be filled solely by the affirmative vote of a majority of the remaining directors in office.
- **No written consent.** Stockholder action may not be taken by written consent in lieu of a meeting, except that holders of Class B common stock, voting separately as a class, may act by written consent.
- **Special meetings.** Special meetings of stockholders may be called only by the Chairman of the Board, the Chief Executive Officer, or the board of directors. Stockholders may not call special meetings.
- **Advance notice.** Our bylaws contain advance notice requirements for stockholder proposals and director nominations.
- **Supermajority amendment.** The affirmative vote of the holders of at least 66⅔% of the voting power is required to amend certain provisions of our charter and bylaws.

## Exclusive Forum

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation will provide that, unless we consent in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware will be the sole and exclusive forum for (i) any derivative action, (ii) any action asserting a breach of fiduciary duty, (iii) any action asserting a claim arising under the DGCL, our charter, or our bylaws, or (iv) any action asserting a claim governed by the internal affairs doctrine. If the Court of Chancery lacks jurisdiction, the federal district court for the District of Delaware will be the exclusive forum. Our charter will also provide that the federal district courts of the United States will be the sole and exclusive forum for the resolution of any complaint asserting a cause of action arising under the Securities Act. These provisions may limit a stockholder's ability to bring a claim in a judicial forum that it finds favorable.

## Corporate Opportunities

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation will renounce our interest in any business opportunity presented to a director who is also a partner, member, director, officer, employee, or agent of any investment fund, venture capital fund, private equity fund, or similar entity, including Graymont Ventures and Ridgeline Growth Partners and their respective affiliates and portfolio companies, unless the opportunity was expressly offered to such director solely in his or her capacity as a director of our company.

## Indemnification

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation and bylaws will provide for the indemnification of our directors and officers to the fullest extent permitted by the DGCL, including advancement of expenses subject to an undertaking to repay. We intend to maintain directors' and officers' liability insurance. Our charter will also eliminate the personal liability of directors and officers for monetary damages for breaches of fiduciary duty to the fullest extent permitted by Section 102(b)(7) of the DGCL.

---

# DESCRIPTION OF INDEBTEDNESS

## Term Loan Facility

In connection with our credit facility, we entered into a senior secured term loan facility with Kestridge West Credit Partners, as lender and administrative agent. The facility provides for borrowings of up to $50.0 million. As of December 31, 2024, $40.0 million was outstanding under the term loan. The term loan bears interest at a variable rate equal to the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR) plus 3.25%, subject to a SOFR floor of 1.00%. Interest is payable quarterly in arrears. The all-in interest rate as of December 31, 2024 was approximately 7.58% per annum. Interest expense related to the term loan was $3.1 million in each of fiscal years 2024 and 2023.

The term loan matures in December 2027. Future scheduled principal repayments as of December 31, 2024 are as follows: $5.0 million in 2025; $10.0 million in 2026; and $25.0 million in 2027. [Drafting note: The Company's internal term loan summary describes level quarterly amortization of 1.25% of the drawn amount ($500,000 per quarter), which is inconsistent with the scheduled repayments above. The amortization schedule must be reconciled with the credit agreement.]

The term loan is secured by a first-priority security interest in substantially all of our assets, including accounts receivable, intellectual property (including our 47 issued U.S. patents, 12 pending applications, 8 foreign patents, and registered trademarks), equipment, deposit accounts, and general intangibles. The security interest in the equity interests of our foreign subsidiary, Crestline Software UK Ltd., is limited to 65% of the voting equity of such subsidiary. Crestline Software UK Ltd. provides a limited guarantee of our obligations under the term loan, capped at £5.0 million.

## Financial Covenants

The credit agreement contains the following financial covenants, tested quarterly:

- **Minimum liquidity.** We must maintain unrestricted cash, cash equivalents, and eligible short-term investments of not less than $30.0 million. As of December 31, 2024, our liquidity was $137.0 million.
- **ARR-based leverage ratio.** The ratio of total funded debt to our annualized recurring revenue must not exceed 2.0x. As of December 31, 2024, our leverage ratio was approximately 0.19x ($40.0 million / $211.6 million ARR).

As of December 31, 2024, we were in compliance with all financial covenants.

## Change of Control

The credit agreement defines "change of control" to include, among other things, the existing stockholders of the Company as of the closing date of the credit agreement ceasing to hold, directly or indirectly, at least 50% of the aggregate voting power of our then-outstanding capital stock, including following an initial public offering. Based on the dual-class capital structure to be adopted upon the closing of this offering, our co-founders are expected to hold approximately 71.8% of the aggregate voting power following this offering, and we do not believe this offering will constitute a change of control. [Drafting note: The change of control analysis in the Company's internal materials equates the "existing stockholders" with the co-founders' Class B voting power. The credit agreement defines "existing stockholders" as the stockholders as of the closing date of the credit agreement, which may include holders other than the co-founders and may exclude holders of Series D preferred stock issued after such date. The analysis should be confirmed against the credit agreement and the lender's interpretation.]

## Public Offering Notice

The credit agreement requires us to provide Kestridge West Credit Partners with written notice not less than 30 days prior to the consummation of any initial public offering of our equity securities. The notice must include the anticipated offering date, anticipated gross proceeds, anticipated use of proceeds (including whether any proceeds will be used to prepay the term loan), and a pro forma capitalization table. Failure to provide this notice within the required timeframe constitutes an event of default, subject to a 10-business-day cure period. We intend to provide this notice at least 35 days prior to the expected pricing date of this offering.

## Events of Default

The credit agreement contains customary events of default, including failure to pay principal or interest, breach of financial covenants, breach of other covenants, material inaccuracy of representations, cross-default to other indebtedness exceeding $2.0 million, bankruptcy, change of control, and judgments for the payment of money in excess of $5.0 million in the aggregate that remain undischarged for 60 days. Upon an event of default, the lender may accelerate all outstanding obligations and exercise remedies with respect to the collateral.

## Other Covenants

The credit agreement contains affirmative and negative covenants that, among other things, restrict our ability to incur additional indebtedness, grant liens, make investments, pay dividends or repurchase stock, and engage in mergers or acquisitions, in each case subject to negotiated thresholds and exceptions. The credit agreement also requires us to notify the lender of any affiliate transaction exceeding $500,000 in value.
S1EOF

# MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

You should read the following discussion together with our consolidated financial statements and the related notes included elsewhere in this prospectus. This discussion contains forward-looking statements that involve risks and uncertainties. Our actual results may differ materially from those anticipated in these forward-looking statements.

## Overview

We are an enterprise SaaS company providing an AI-driven supply chain optimization platform. We generate revenue primarily from subscriptions to our cloud-based platform, with a smaller portion from professional services. Subscription revenue is recognized ratably over the contractual term, typically one to three years. Professional services revenue is recognized as services are delivered. For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024, subscription revenue accounted for 90.0% of total revenue and professional services revenue accounted for 10.0%.

## Key Operating Metrics

We monitor the following key operating metrics to measure and manage our business:

- **Annual recurring revenue (ARR).** ARR was $211.6 million as of December 31, 2024, compared to $148.2 million as of December 31, 2023, representing growth of 42.8%.
- **Dollar-based net retention rate.** Our dollar-based net retention rate was 132% for fiscal year 2024.
- **Customers.** We served 487 customers as of December 31, 2024, including 34 customers with ARR in excess of $1.0 million.
- **Remaining performance obligations.** As of December 31, 2024, we had remaining performance obligations of approximately $285.0 million, of which approximately 62% is expected to be recognized as revenue over the next 12 months.

## Components of Results of Operations

**Revenue.** We generate subscription revenue and professional services revenue. Subscription revenue consists primarily of fees from access to our cloud-based platform. Professional services revenue consists of implementation, configuration, training, and optimization consulting.

**Cost of revenue.** Subscription cost of revenue consists primarily of third-party hosting and infrastructure costs, personnel-related costs, and amortization of capitalized software development costs. Professional services cost of revenue consists primarily of personnel-related costs and subcontractor costs.

**Operating expenses.** Our operating expenses consist of research and development, sales and marketing, and general and administrative. We expense research and development costs as incurred, except for certain qualifying internal-use software development costs, which are capitalized.

## Results of Operations

The following table sets forth our results of operations for the fiscal years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023 (in thousands):

| | FY 2024 | FY 2023 | $ Change | % Change |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Subscription revenue | $168,600 | $116,400 | $52,200 | 44.8% |
| Professional services revenue | 18,700 | 12,500 | 6,200 | 49.6% |
| Total revenue | 187,300 | 128,900 | 58,400 | 45.3% |
| Subscription cost of revenue | 37,400 | 25,800 | 11,600 | 45.0% |
| Professional services cost of revenue | 18,800 | 15,500 | 3,300 | 21.3% |
| Total cost of revenue | 56,200 | 41,300 | 14,900 | 36.1% |
| Gross profit | 131,100 | 87,600 | 43,500 | 49.7% |
| Research and development | 62,800 | 45,200 | 17,600 | 38.9% |
| Sales and marketing | 71,400 | 52,100 | 19,300 | 37.0% |
| General and administrative | 24,900 | 21,400 | 3,500 | 16.4% |
| Total operating expenses | 159,100 | 118,700 | 40,400 | 34.0% |
| Loss from operations | (28,000) | (31,100) | 3,100 | 10.0% |
| Interest income | 6,200 | 5,800 | 400 | 6.9% |
| Interest expense | (3,100) | (3,100) | — | — |
| Other income, net | 400 | 1,100 | (700) | (63.6)% |
| Net loss before income taxes | (24,500) | (27,300) | 2,800 | 10.3% |

**Revenue.** Total revenue increased $58.4 million, or 45.3%, to $187.3 million in fiscal year 2024 from $128.9 million in fiscal year 2023. The increase was driven by new customer acquisition and expansion within our existing customer base. Subscription revenue increased $52.2 million, or 44.8%, and professional services revenue increased $6.2 million, or 49.6%.

**Cost of revenue and gross margin.** Total cost of revenue increased $14.9 million, or 36.1%, to $56.2 million. Gross profit increased $43.5 million, or 49.7%, to $131.1 million, and our gross margin increased to 70.0% in fiscal year 2024 from 67.9% in fiscal year 2023. Subscription gross margin was 77.8% in fiscal year 2024. Professional services gross margin was approximately (0.5)% in fiscal year 2024, as professional services cost of revenue ($18.8 million) slightly exceeded professional services revenue ($18.7 million). We expect professional services to remain a modest but strategically important component of total revenue, and we are implementing measures to improve professional services efficiency over time.

**Operating expenses.** Total operating expenses increased $40.4 million, or 34.0%, to $159.1 million. Research and development expense increased $17.6 million, or 38.9%, reflecting investment in our engineering team and platform development. Sales and marketing expense increased $19.3 million, or 37.0%, reflecting expansion of our direct sales organization. General and administrative expense increased $3.5 million, or 16.4%. Stock-based compensation expense totaled $38.2 million in fiscal year 2024 and $26.4 million in fiscal year 2023, and is included in cost of revenue and each operating expense category.

**Loss from operations.** Loss from operations decreased $3.1 million, or 10.0%, to $28.0 million, reflecting operating leverage as revenue growth outpaced operating expense growth.

**Interest income and expense.** Interest income was $6.2 million in fiscal year 2024, an increase of $0.4 million, reflecting higher average cash and investment balances. Interest expense was $3.1 million in each of fiscal years 2024 and 2023, related to our term loan.

**Net loss.** Net loss decreased $2.6 million, or 9.5%, to $24.8 million.

## Non-GAAP Financial Measure: Adjusted EBITDA

We present Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP financial measure, as a supplemental measure of our operating performance. We define Adjusted EBITDA as net loss adjusted to exclude depreciation and amortization, stock-based compensation, interest income, interest expense, income tax provision, and restructuring charges. Adjusted EBITDA is not a measure of financial performance under GAAP and should not be considered as an alternative to net loss or any other GAAP measure. As of December 31, 2024, we had $94.7 million in cash and cash equivalents and $42.3 million in short-term investments, for total liquidity of $137.0 million. We have funded our operations primarily through the issuance of redeemable convertible preferred stock, revenue from operations, and borrowings under our term loan. We have incurred net losses since inception and had an accumulated deficit of $312.6 million as of December 31, 2024. Management believes that existing cash resources, together with anticipated cash from operations, will be sufficient to fund operations for at least 12 months from the date of issuance of our consolidated financial statements.

**Cash flows.** Net cash provided by operating activities was $29.4 million in fiscal year 2024 and $12.9 million in fiscal year 2023. Net cash used in investing activities was $11.9 million in fiscal year 2024 and $211.7 million in fiscal year 2023. Net cash used in financing activities was $0.8 million in fiscal year 2024, while net cash provided by financing activities was $258.7 million in fiscal year 2023 (primarily from the issuance of Series D preferred stock).

| **Total** | **$84,600** | **$15,700** | **$19,900** | **$34,800** | **$7,100** | **$7,100** |

## Critical Accounting Policies and Estimates

Our consolidated financial statements are prepared in accordance with GAAP. Our independent registered public accounting firm has identified revenue recognition for arrangements with multiple performance obligations and the valuation of common stock underlying stock-based compensation awards as critical audit matters.

# BUSINESS

## Our Company

Crestline Software, Inc. is an enterprise software-as-a-service company providing an AI-driven supply chain optimization platform to Fortune 1000 manufacturers and retailers. Our platform, anchored by the Crestline Nexus platform and our proprietary SupplyMind AI engine, combines machine learning, advanced statistical modeling, and real-time data integration to deliver predictive and prescriptive supply chain intelligence, including demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and logistics routing. We were incorporated in Delaware on March 14, 2017 and are headquartered in Austin, Texas.

## Our Products and Technology

Our platform consists of the Crestline Nexus platform and the SupplyMind AI engine. The SupplyMind AI engine uses deep learning and reinforcement learning models trained on billions of supply chain events to provide demand sensing, safety stock optimization, network design, and logistics routing. The platform features a multi-tenant, cloud-native architecture with real-time data ingestion from enterprise resource planning, warehouse management, transportation management, and IoT systems, with pre-built connectors for over 100 enterprise data sources.

## Our Customers

We served 487 customers as of December 31, 2024, across manufacturing, retail, logistics, and consumer packaged goods. Our largest customer, Harmon Industries Inc., represented approximately 8.2% of total revenue in fiscal year 2024. Our top 10 customers collectively represented 31.7% of total revenue. No single customer other than Harmon Industries exceeded 5.0% of total revenue. Revenue is well diversified across verticals, with manufacturing representing approximately 38%, retail 24%, logistics 19%, and consumer packaged goods 12% of revenue.

## Our Intellectual Property

Our intellectual property portfolio includes 47 issued U.S. patents, 12 pending U.S. patent applications, and 8 issued foreign patents covering core algorithmic innovations and data processing methods. We have registered the trademarks "Crestline," "Crestline Nexus," and "SupplyMind" with the United States Patent and Trademark Office. We also rely on trade secrets and proprietary training data.

## Competition

The market for supply chain optimization software is competitive and includes legacy enterprise resource planning vendors and point-solution providers. We compete on the basis of our AI-native platform, intellectual property, customer outcomes, and enterprise scalability.

## Employees

As of December 31, 2024, we had 1,247 employees. During the third quarter of 2024, we implemented a workforce reduction affecting approximately 46 employees and recorded restructuring charges of $1.2 million.

## Properties

We lease office facilities in Austin, Texas (headquarters, expiring August 2029, annual base rent approximately $3.8 million); San Francisco, California (expiring March 2027, annual base rent approximately $2.1 million); London, United Kingdom (expiring January 2028, annual base rent approximately £680,000); and Bangalore, India (expiring June 2026, annual base rent approximately INR 42 million). We believe our facilities are adequate for our current needs.

## Legal Proceedings

See "Legal Proceedings" for a description of the pending patent infringement litigation.

## Corporate History and Structure

We were incorporated in Delaware on March 14, 2017. We operate through our wholly-owned subsidiary, Crestline Software UK Ltd., incorporated in England and Wales. We have raised an aggregate of $385.0 million through the issuance of redeemable convertible preferred stock across four financing rounds: Series A ($12.0 million, October 2018); Series B ($38.0 million, April 2020); Series C ($95.0 million, November 2021); and Series D ($240.0 million, June 2023). The Series D financing closed at $24.00 per share, implying a post-money equity valuation of approximately $1.92 billion.
S1EOF

## Executive Officer and Director Biographies

**Arun Ramaswamy** is our Co-Founder, Chief Executive Officer, and Chairman of the Board, and has served in such roles since our incorporation in March 2017. Prior to founding Crestline, Mr. Ramaswamy served as an Engagement Manager at McKinsey & Company. He holds a B.S. in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology and an M.B.A. from the Stanford Graduate School of Business.

**Katerina "Kate" Litvak** is our Co-Founder and Chief Technology Officer, and has served in such roles since our incorporation in March 2017. Prior to co-founding Crestline, Dr. Litvak was a researcher at a major technology company's artificial intelligence research laboratory. She holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon University.

**Michael D. Hartwell** is our Chief Financial Officer, a position he has held since 2020. Prior to joining Crestline, Mr. Hartwell served as Vice President of Finance at a publicly traded enterprise software company. Priya Venkatesh, a member of our board of directors, is the Managing Partner of Graymont Ventures and serves on the board of directors of Vantara Logistics. The transaction was reviewed and approved by our Audit Committee, with Ms. Venkatesh recused from the deliberation and vote. The Audit Committee concluded that the terms of the agreement were comparable to those offered to similarly situated customers. Revenue recognized from Vantara was $1.2 million during fiscal year 2024 and $0.9 million during fiscal year 2023. Accounts receivable from Vantara as of December 31, 2024 and 2023 were $0.3 million and $0.25 million, respectively.

# LEGAL PROCEEDINGS

From time to time, we are involved in legal proceedings, claims, and regulatory matters arising in the ordinary course of business. We are not currently a party to any legal proceedings that we believe will have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows, other than the matter described below.

On September 12, 2024, Optivance, Inc. filed a patent infringement lawsuit against us in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Texas (Case No. 6:24-cv-00831), alleging that certain features of our SupplyMind platform infringe U.S. Patent No. 11,284,107, relating to "machine-learning-based inventory forecasting." We believe the claims are without merit and intend to vigorously defend against them. The lawsuit is in the early stages of litigation, and no trial date has been set. We have not recorded a loss contingency for this matter because management believes it is not probable that a loss has been incurred. Based on the advice of outside patent counsel, we estimate the potential range of loss, in the event of an adverse judgment, to be between $5.0 million and $20.0 million, although an unfavorable outcome is considered to have a low probability. An adverse judgment in excess of $5.0 million that remains undischarged for 60 days could constitute an event of default under our term loan. See "Description of Indebtedness."

# LEGAL PROCEEDINGS

From time to time, we are involved in legal proceedings, claims, and regulatory matters arising in the ordinary course of business. We are not currently a party to any legal proceedings that we believe will have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows, other than the matter described below.

On September 12, 2024, Optivance, Inc. filed a patent infringement lawsuit against us in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Texas (Case No. 6:24-cv-00831), alleging that certain features of our SupplyMind platform infringe U.S. Patent No. 11,284,107, relating to "machine-learning-based inventory forecasting." We believe the claims are without merit and intend to vigorously defend against them. The lawsuit is in the early stages of litigation, and no trial date has been set. We have not recorded a loss contingency for this matter because management believes it is not probable that a loss has been incurred. Based on the advice of outside patent counsel, we estimate the potential range of loss, in the event of an adverse judgment, to be between $5.0 million and $20.0 million, although an unfavorable outcome is considered to have a low probability. An adverse judgment in excess of $5.0 million that remains undischarged for 60 days could constitute an event of default under our term loan. See "Description of Indebtedness."
